proposals.content:
### Sableclutch Scrap

Sableclutch Scrap is a large scrapyard and junkyard business located within the neighborhood. It is an expansive site featuring mountains of trash and industrial equipment, including a crane-like device used to flatten vehicles. The yard is run by a proprietor known as [[Copperjaw]], and has a reputation for giving jobs to anyone willing to do the work.

### Character of the District

The streets of Sableclutch are relatively winding. The district contains factories and scrapyards, with trucks regularly carting goods in and out of them, and is served by a trolley station. Goblinoid creatures are commonly employed as workers in the local factories and can be seen commuting through the streets in workman's attire. The area lacks traffic cops — a reflection of the district's neglected status, as there is no clear party willing to fund them.

proposals.oldText: Calarian radio shows and music broadcast from every antenna
proposals.newText: Calarian radio shows and music broadcast from every antenna (there is no television in Færrin — radio is the dominant broadcast medium)

proposals.oldText:
> [!note] The Mundane Færrish
> Before magic was introduced to Færrin, the only sentient life on the planet was human. Afterward, more than half of the humans found themselves taking on new traits, spurred by the sudden influx of magical energy. Magic beasts with sentience also appeared throughout the planet. Perhaps this is unimportant to modern life, but it's an interesting tidbit for researchers trying to find what encourages intelligence in the Astra.
proposals.newText:
> [!note] The Mundane Færrish
> Before magic was introduced to Færrin, the only sentient life on the planet was human. Afterward, more than half of the humans found themselves taking on new traits, spurred by the sudden influx of magical energy. Magic beasts with sentience also appeared throughout the planet. Perhaps this is unimportant to modern life, but it's an interesting tidbit for researchers trying to find what encourages intelligence in the Astra.

Today, Færrin is a fully magical society. Seeing bird people and other non-human beings going about their lives is commonplace and does not attract unusual attention.

proposals.content:
> [!note] Current Status
> Raelion no longer exists — the country has been destroyed.

proposals.oldText: Now, a member of Belvedere has a few paths to take. They could join the ranks of the academics, studying natural phenomena. They could become archivists, recording history's ebb and flow. They could be aristocrats, moving through polite society like a fish through water. Finally, they could be arcanists, working closely with the Hearts and their divine machinery.
proposals.newText:
Now, a member of Belvedere has a few paths to take. They could join the ranks of the academics, studying natural phenomena. They could become archivists, recording history's ebb and flow. They could be aristocrats, moving through polite society like a fish through water. Finally, they could be arcanists, working closely with the Hearts and their divine machinery.

Belvedere also functions as a school, providing education to children within its walls. Children are not ordinarily granted permission to travel hundreds of miles away into the field — such cases are treated as special exceptions.

proposals.content:
### Contacts & Job Leads

Anouk maintains a Rolodex-like collection of index cards containing contacts and job leads, which she uses to refer people to work opportunities. Among her contacts is an old friend who runs [[Sableclutch]] Scrap — known as [[Copperjaw]] — who regularly hires couriers and other workers, preferring to engage individuals directly rather than through a courier service.

proposals.oldText: Illmari is the current proprietor of the [[Sin and Tonic]] and the founder of the mercenary group [[Org/Iconoclasm/index|Iconoclasm]].
proposals.newText: Illmari is the current proprietor of the [[Sin and Tonic]] and the founder of the mercenary group [[Org/Iconoclasm/index|Iconoclasm]]. He normally serves as the cook at the establishment, while [[Ginny]] works the bar.

proposals.content: Pyrelight operates through a front company known as [[Embercall Industries]].

proposals.oldText: He is the founder and leader of the [[Org/Roundhat Gang/index|Roundhat Gang]], a protection racket in [[Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/index|Sableclutch]]. He was rumored to have gone crazy, hearing voices, but it appears that he's struck a deal with someone in [[Org/Pyrelight/index|Pyrelight]]. Formerly unable to wield magic, he now pilots a simulacrum to work on his behalf.
proposals.newText: He is the founder and leader of the [[Org/Roundhat Gang/index|Roundhat Gang]], a protection racket in [[Geography/Calaria/Hallia/Sableclutch/index|Sableclutch]]. He was rumored to have gone crazy, hearing voices, but it appears that he's struck a deal with someone in [[Org/Pyrelight/index|Pyrelight]], and has ordered materials from [[Embercall Industries]], a front company of that faction. Formerly unable to wield magic, he now pilots a simulacrum to work on his behalf.

proposals.oldText: - **Arbiters** track down those who have escaped justice and deliver them their due.
proposals.newText: - **Arbiters** track down those who have escaped justice and deliver them their due. The Scale does not extend this authority to unaffiliated individuals — bounty hunting is a power reserved for Arbiters alone.

proposals.content:
---
title: Anzu
---
Anzu has a raven companion named Othello.

---
title: Argyle
---
Argyle's parents are [[Aaron Cross]] and [[Joran Davinrock]].

---
title: Blackline
---
Blackline is an organization whose personnel carry physical identification badges bearing an identification number.

Blackline badges are not widely recognized by local law enforcement, owing to the organization's infrequent interactions with them.

---
tags:
  - Dwarf
---
Copperjaw is an elderly dwarf, estimated to be around 120 to 130 years old — at the end of middle age or the beginning of elderly by dwarven standards. His bottom jaw has been replaced with a crude copper prosthetic, which is the origin of his name.

He operates out of a scrapyard, where he has access to a Giant crusher and feels confident deterring interested parties who come looking for trouble. Outside of his scrapyard, however, he does not feel confident protecting valuable items.

### Work and Courier Jobs

Copperjaw offers work in two broad categories: guarding something or moving something, with moving jobs paying better at 100 gold pieces. He has been known to hire people — including children — to deliver important items or cases.

For delivery jobs, Copperjaw uses a locked metal suitcase fitted with a magic handcuff on its handle, used to secure the case to a courier. The suitcase is extraordinarily heavy — heavy enough that dropping it would make a small crater in the ground.

### Connections

Copperjaw is a named individual known to [[Org/Protectorate/index|Blackline]] operatives, who would be informed if a goblin of interest were caught.

---
tags:
  - Protectorate
---
Mute is the partner of [[Org/Protectorate/People/Officer Smith|Officer Smith]], a fellow operative who is unable to speak due to a bad accident sustained in a scuffle with a local gang. In place of speech, Mute communicates only through a gargling sound.

Like Smith, Mute is a shapeshifting creature capable of disguising itself as an ordinary office worker to blend into urban environments. When dropping this disguise, Mute grows approximately a foot and a half in size, and its clothing melts back into its body. In their true form, both Mute and Smith appear as large, pink, fleshy, naked mole rat-like creatures, with eyes that sink into featureless sockets and most other facial features dissolving back into the skin.

---
title: Officer Smith
---
Officer Smith is a named officer of the local law enforcement.

---
title: Prime
aliases:
  - Zero
---
Prime (also called Zero) is a tram station serving as a destination on the numbered tram network, which includes stations such as [[Void 2]].

---
title: Radar Dish
---
A device believed to be used for receiving a signal.

Activating the radar dish is considered particularly dangerous in the [[<UNKNOWN>|NPC craftsman's workshop]], where [[rocket hammer]]s are made.

---
title: Skinwalkers
---
Skinwalkers are difficult to identify by sight alone, requiring a significantly higher DC than identifying whether someone is mute through conversation.

---
tags:
  - Centaur
---
Smiler is a centaur standing approximately nine feet tall at full height, forcing him to stoop beneath the low ceiling of his establishment. He is the proprietor of the [[Undertable]] and operates as a [[fence]], dealing in both buying and selling goods.

Smiler is known to [[Johnny]] and extends a friends discount to Johnny's associates as a personal favor, on the basis that friends of his friend will not inform on him to law enforcement. His pawn shop stocks items such as poly tools, priced at nine gold pieces.

---
title: The Twins
aliases:
  - the twins
---
Two individuals known for their exceptional crafting ability. They are not shopkeepers, but can craft [[healing potions]] if provided with the necessary components.

---
title: The Undertable
---
A pawn shop tucked away enough to avoid drawing attention, yet not so remote as to be difficult to reach.

---
title: Threshold Authority
aliases:
  - TA
tags:
  - Law Enforcement
---
The Threshold Authority (commonly abbreviated **TA**) is the local law enforcement organization operating in the area.

TA officers are identifiable by their greenish uniform bearing a large **TA** insignia.

---
title: Tram
aliases:
  - Trolley
  - Trams
  - Trolleys
---
The local transit system uses trams (also called trolleys) that materialize at stations on a scheduled basis.

---
title: Undertable
---
A narrow, squished shop wedged between two buildings in a space too large for an alleyway but too small for a standard building. The Undertable has no shop-front windows, and its entrance is marked only by a door bearing a handwritten sign reading *under table* alongside a crude drawing of a table.

The interior is a cramped room filled with tables stacked high with miscellaneous goods, including kitchen appliances, loose jewelry, radios in various states of deconstruction, and a large number of firearms.

proposals.message:
Claim: Augers are creatures created through a divination ritual and can be fed to enhance divination.
Rationale: No candidate wiki page matched this claim.

Claim: A Fane is a structure whose existence and size are sustained by a magical object called a heart.
Rationale: The page describes a fane as the heart's home/dwelling place, not as a structure sustained by a magical object called a heart; rather, the heart lives *in* the fane and controls it, and the heart itself is the entity (not a magical object).
Page excerpt: hearts live in a place called a *fane*. its lik their house but its smaller on the outside and they have lots of control in it.

Claim: Multiple hearts can jointly maintain a single Fane, a practice used by large organizational headquarters to sustain large footprints.
Rationale: The page describes multiple small fanes merging into one big fane so hearts can coexist, not multiple hearts jointly maintaining a single fane from the outset; the mechanism is merging of separate fanes, not joint maintenance.
Page excerpt: sometimes a bunch of small fanes merge into a big 1 and then all the hearts can hang out!

Claim: Belvedere has integrated hearts as an additional tier within its social or civic hierarchy, and Fanes are more common there than in most other places.
Rationale: The page confirms Hearts are integrated into Belvedere's hierarchy, but makes no mention of Fanes being more common there than elsewhere — and the page only describes Hearts as 'slivers of sentient divine energy gifted by the Outer Gods,' not Fanes.
Page excerpt: the Hearts became coordinators and leaders within the Org, and in the modern day, many report to a Heart alongside (or _instead of_) another person.

Claim: The RunGunners are a particularly famous group of mercenaries and guns-for-hire.
Rationale: The page describes the RunGunners as an armored transport/delivery organization, not mercenaries or guns-for-hire, so characterizing them primarily as such conflicts with the page's description.
Page excerpt: The RunGunners toe the line between upstanding armored transport and hooligans with too-fast cars and too-itchy trigger fingers. If you need something (or someone) to get somewhere in one piece, you hire a RunGunner convoy.

Claim: Ginny's appearance consists of a suit vest and dress pants, but instead of a head, Ginny has a twisted-up dish rag.
Rationale: The page describes Ginny as wearing a three-piece suit (not just a vest and dress pants), and the dishrag forms his face by scrunching up rather than replacing a head entirely, making the claim's description inaccurate on both counts.
Page excerpt: Gin Soaked Rag, or Ginny for short, appears as a 6-foot-tall dishrag wearing a three-piece suit... His face is formed by the dishrag scrunching up.

Claim: Sableclutch is notable for having a large number of religious areas, including many churches.
Rationale: The page makes no mention of churches or religious areas in Sableclutch; it describes the district as dominated by dockworkers and warehouse employees, with no indication of notable religious presence.
Page excerpt: Sableclutch is dominated by the dockworkers and warehouse employees that ply their trade on the river.
